evals.expected_output: Simplified version using guard clauses, named constants, extracted helper functions, proper TypeScript types, filter/map instead of manual loop, and clear naming.

assertions.name: reduces_nesting
assertions.description: Maximum nesting depth reduced from 5 to 2 or less

assertions.name: uses_guard_clauses_or_filter
assertions.description: Uses early returns, filter(), or guard clauses instead of nested ifs

assertions.name: extracts_constants
assertions.description: 86400000 replaced with a named constant like MS_PER_DAY

assertions.name: adds_types
assertions.description: Replaces 'any' with proper TypeScript interface/type

assertions.name: extracts_helper
assertions.description: Name formatting logic extracted to a separate function

assertions.name: uses_modern_syntax
assertions.description: Uses const/let, template literals, or array methods instead of manual loop

evals.expected_output: Flat error handling with single try/except chain or extracted helper, reduced nesting, DRY error result construction

assertions.name: reduces_nesting
assertions.description: Nesting reduced from 5 levels to 2 or fewer

assertions.name: extracts_error_handler
assertions.description: Error result construction extracted to a helper or uses a consistent pattern

assertions.name: removes_duplication
assertions.description: The repeated results.append({url, data:None, status, error}) pattern is DRY

assertions.name: preserves_error_specificity
assertions.description: Still distinguishes between timeout, parse, validation, and request errors

assertions.name: uses_single_try_except
assertions.description: Uses a single try/except with multiple except clauses instead of nested try blocks

evals.expected_output: Extracted custom hook useAsync or useFetch, single StatCard component, reduced from 9 useState to 0-3 via custom hook

assertions.name: extracts_custom_hook
assertions.description: Extracts a reusable useAsync/useFetch hook that handles data, loading, and error states

assertions.name: reduces_state_count
assertions.description: Reduces from 9 useState calls to 3 or fewer via the custom hook

assertions.name: extracts_reusable_component
assertions.description: Extracts a StatCard or similar component for the repeated loading/error/data pattern

assertions.name: removes_jsx_duplication
assertions.description: The three identical ternary blocks replaced with a map or reusable component

assertions.name: preserves_behavior
assertions.description: All three data sources still fetched independently with loading/error states

evals.expected_output: Replace switch with a Record/Map lookup, extract type from status code range, derive retryable from a Set

assertions.name: replaces_switch_with_map
assertions.description: Uses a Record, Map, or object lookup instead of switch statement

assertions.name: reduces_line_count
assertions.description: Total lines reduced by at least 30%

assertions.name: handles_default_case
assertions.description: Unknown status codes still return a fallback value

assertions.name: preserves_all_mappings
assertions.description: All 17 status codes still map to their correct label, type, and retryable value

assertions.name: adds_type_safety
assertions.description: Uses TypeScript types for the return shape and/or satisfies keyword

evals.expected_output: Flat async/await with try/catch, email failure handled separately, no .then nesting

assertions.name: converts_to_async_await
assertions.description: Function uses async/await instead of .then() chains

assertions.name: removes_nesting
assertions.description: No more than 1 level of nesting in the function body

assertions.name: preserves_error_handling
assertions.description: Outer errors still caught, email failure still non-fatal

assertions.name: uses_try_catch
assertions.description: Uses try/catch for error handling instead of .catch()

assertions.name: maintains_sequential_logic
assertions.description: Steps still execute in order: get order, validate, pay, update, email
